note: WP 5.4 live staging evidence, uvicorn-preview :8765, agent-login + active_team=Nationalkader, http://127.0.0.1:8765/play/infinity/?source=mask&file=35&filename=20250605_114059.jpg (initial page). Because the preview tab is backgrounded (document.visibilityState='hidden'), rAF-driven rendering and the phase-gated in-game stats toggle can't be exercised directly (known repo limitation, see wp5_3_summary.md). Instead this probe drives the REAL production MaskSceneSource class (dynamic import of the unmodified /static/results/js/infinite/mask_scene_source.js module, which owns the real worker.js) directly from the page's console, simulating repeated scene consumption at the ~2s play cadence documented in PREFETCH_TARGET. This exercises the exact same worker.js/navgraph_router.js/route_pair_selection.js/refine_theta.js code path a real play session would.

file35.name: Solothurn
file35.note: metrics.maxPairMs=5357.5ms (wall-clock incl. worker-queue wait, measured by mask_scene_source.js) exceeded PAIR_WARN_MS(5000) twice (metrics.slow=2), confirmed live by console.warn('[mask-source] buffer low: ...') firing repeatedly whenever buffer dropped to 1. No individual generatePair computation (meta.workerMs, measured inside worker.js) exceeded 5000ms in the captured 25-pair sample (max 3869.1ms) - the two 'slow' wall-clock events came from queueing behind other in-flight requests on the single-threaded worker under PREFETCH_TARGET=3 concurrent demand, not a single overrun computation. All values stayed far below the 20000ms PAIR_TIMEOUT_MS hard guard.

file37.name: Aarberg
file37.note: Cleanest run: buffer held at 2 ready (bufAfter) for essentially the whole 12-pair/2s-cadence simulation (dipped to 1 once during warm-up at i=1), zero slow pairs, 100% theta refinement (no legal-spine fallbacks needed).

file142.name: Locarno Sprint Floli
file142.note_enablement: infinite_enabled=False and infinite_region unset on this File; the raw /editor/navgraph/, /editor/mask/, /editor/level-passages/ endpoints (which MaskSceneSource uses) are not gated by infinite_enabled (that flag only gates the map picker's infinite_mask_maps listing), so this 75Mpx mask - the same one used for WP5.2/5.3 Node acceptance - could be driven directly for verification without touching the model.
file142.note: Worst-case map size. metrics.maxPairMs=8062.8ms wall-clock (queueing-inflated under 3 concurrent prefetch requests on a single worker thread) - comfortably under the 20000ms PAIR_TIMEOUT_MS guard. No individual generatePair computation exceeded 5000ms in the captured sample (max meta.workerMs 3563.3ms). Higher legal-fallback rate on this map (matches WP5.2's own 75Mpx acceptance note about p90 refine time being the Phase-6 tuning outlier) plus likely additional CPU throttling from the backgrounded preview tab (Chrome deprioritizes hidden-tab renderer processes; see 'Preview tab rAF frozen' repo note) - not a WP5.4 wiring defect, refineTimeoutPolicy:'fallback' served every pair successfully.

consoleEvidence.capturedWarnings: [warn] [mask-source] buffer low: 1 ready, 1 in flight  (x40+, fired live during the loops above whenever buffer.length after takeScene() < 2)
consoleEvidence.workerThreadConsoleNotCaptured: worker.js's own '[theta-client] generatePair slow: ...' console.warn (fired when dt > PAIR_WARN_MS inside the dedicated Worker) does not surface through this browser tool's console capture - a CDP/tooling limitation (dedicated Worker execution contexts are a separate target this simplified preview harness does not attach to), not a code defect. Equivalent proof is deterministic from meta.workerMs itself: worker.js computes `res.meta.workerMs = +dt.toFixed(2)` from the exact same `dt` tested by `if (dt > PAIR_WARN_MS) console.warn(...)` immediately prior, so any workerMs > 5000 in the captured data is proof-by-construction that the warning branch executed.

statsPanelDomEvidence.method: Reproduced the literal renderStatsPanel() mask-diagnostics block (results/static/results/js/infinite_play.js lines ~3811-3838, unmodified) against REAL captured meta objects from the probes above, executed in the live page (real gettext() catalog, real #play-stats-panel DOM node, real play.css). Done via DOM injection rather than the in-game phase-gated stats-toggle click handler, because that handler requires phase==='reveal' with a live game `scene` (module-internal, not exposed to window) reached only via a full route-choice play-through, which rAF-frozen background tabs cannot complete (known repo limitation).
statsPanelDomEvidence.renderedHtml: Mask generationAttempts1Retries0Refinelegal-fallback (fallback)Rejections0Timingssample 0ms, snap 0ms, route 61ms, refine 37ms, theta 1201ms
statsPanelDomEvidence.note: Non-zero bounding box confirms .stats-mask-diagnostics / .stats-diagnostics-title / .stats-row / .stats-label / .stats-value CSS (results/static/results/css/play.css) is present post-collectstatic and renders visibly. preview_screenshot timed out (rAF-frozen hidden tab - same known limitation as WP5.3), so DOM/style probing substitutes for a screenshot per the accepted repo pattern.

barrierThreadingEvidence.note: Confirms mask_scene_source.js's _wrapPair barrier/skippedBarrier conversion to map units (x TRAIN_SCALE_VALUE) is unaffected by the WP5.4 changes; buildMaskScene threads pair.barriers/pair.skippedBarriers into scene.routeResult exactly as WP5.3 already live-verified against drawRouteBlocks (scratch/wp5_3/live_pair_file35.json).
